sourceMovie.title: Letters to Juliet
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Letters to Juliet (2010) as a comedy theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The comedy and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Hitch
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Letters to Juliet clicked for you, Hitch (2005) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Letters to Juliet?
faq.answer: Try Hitch, Sex and the City, Moonstruck—each shares DNA with Letters to Juliet while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Letters to Juliet?
faq.answer: Letters to Juliet (2010) is best described as Comedy, Drama, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Letters to Juliet worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
